REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF UNITED BUSINESS BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER INSTRUCTING AND AUTHORIZING RECEIVER TO LIST AND MARKET 410 EDDY STREET, SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA FOR SALE; AND CONFIRMING SALE PROCEDURES IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS DEFAULTED ON DECEMBER 21, 2022. PLAINTIFF COULD HAVE OBTAINED A JUDGMENT IN THIS ACTION IN DEPARTMENT 514 AT ANY TIME THEREAFTER. THERE IS NO INDICATION IN THE REGISTER OF ACTIONS THAT PLAINTIFF ATTEMPTED TO OR SCHEDULED A HEARING TO OBTAIN A JUDGMENT EVEN THOUGH SEVERAL CASE MANAGEMENT ORDERS WERE ISSUED BY DEPARTMENT 610 FOR PLAINTIFF TO OBTAIN A DEFAULT JUDGMENT BY THE TIME OF THE CMC. NO AUTHORITY IS PROVIDED FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT THE REQUESTED RELIEF IS NECESSARY AND PROPER IN LIGHT OF THE PROCEDURAL POSTURE OF THIS CASE. PLAINTIFF SHALL PROCEED WITH DEFAULT JUDGMENT FORTHWITH TO ADDRESS THE PENDING CMC ORDER. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

NOTICE NOTICE OF NO OPPOSITION FILED TO JOINT MOTION OF KEVIN SINGER, RECEIVER, AND PLAINTIFF UNITED BUSINESS BANK FOR ORDER: (1) INSTRUCTING AND AUTHORIZING RECEIVER TO LIST AND MARKET 410 EDDY STREET, SAN FRANCISCO, CA FOR SALE; AND (2) CONFIRMING SALE PROCEDURES (TRANSACTION ID # 100215138) FILED BY PLAINTIFF UNITED BUSINESS BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, JOINT MOTION OF KEVIN SINGER, RECEIVER, AND PLAINTIFF UMPQUA BANK FOR ORDER (1) INSTRUCTING AND AUTHORIZING RECEIVER TO LIST AND MARKET 410 EDDY STREET, SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA FOR SALE AND (2) CONFIRMING SALE PROCEDURES IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF OBTAINED A FINAL JUDGMENT ON THE SOLE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE AND APPOINTMENT OF A RECEIVER IN THIS ACTION. PER THE TERMS OF THE LOAN DOCUMENTS, THE RECEIVER IS IN PLACE "TO TAKE POSSESSION OF ALL OR ANY PART OF THE PROPERTY, WITH THE POWER TO PROTECT AND PRESERVE THE PROPERTY, TO OPERATE THE PROPERTY PRECEDING FORECLOSURE OR SALE, AND TO COLLECT THE RENTS FROM THE PROPERTY, AND APPLY THE PROCEEDS, OVER AND ABOVE THE COSTS OF THE RECEIVERSHIP, AGAINST THE INDEBTEDNESS." SINCE THE RELIEF REQUESTED IN THIS ACTION HAS BEEN OBTAIN IN FULL, PLAINTIFF SHALL PROCEED WITH ITS OTHER REMEDIES AVAILABLE UNDER THE LAW OR CONTRACT WITH "FORECLOSURE OR SALE" WITHIN THE MEANING OF THE CONTRACT. THE COURT WILL NOT AUTHORIZE THE RECEIVER TO PROCEED WITH THE SALE ON A MATTER REDUCED TO JUDGEMENT ON THE RELIEF REQUESTED. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Notice Of Joint Motion Of Kevin Singer, Receiver, And Plaintiff Umpqua Bank For Order (1) Instructing And Authorizing Receiver To List And Market 410 Eddy Street, San Francisco, California For Sale And (2) Confirming Sale Procedures
Off Calendar Oct-12-2023 Unknown
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Robert B. Kaplan; Jeffer Mangels Butler & Mitchell LLP; 415-398-8080; appearing as counsel for plaintiff United Business Bank.
Kevin Singer; 415-590-4823; Superior Court Receiver, appearing in pro per.

Plaintiff contests the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

JOINT MOTION OF KEVIN SINGER, RECEIVER, AND PLAINTIFF UMPQUA BANK FOR ORDER (1) INSTRUCTING AND AUTHORIZING RECEIVER TO LIST AND MARKET 410 EDDY STREET, SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA FOR SALE AND (2) CONFIRMING SALE PROCEDURES is DENIED. Plaintiff obtained a final judgment on the sole cause of action for specific performance and appointment of a receiver in this action. Per the terms of the loan documents, the receiver is in place "to take possession of all or any part of the Property, with the power to protect and preserve the Property, to operate the property preceding foreclosure or sale, and to collect the Rents from the Property, and apply the proceeds, over and above the costs of the receivership, against the Indebtedness." Since the relief requested in this action has been obtain in full, Plaintiff shall proceed with its other remedies available under the law or contract with "foreclosure or sale" within the meaning of the contract. The Court will not authorize the receiver to proceed with the sale on a matter reduced to judgement on the relief requested.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Instructing And Authorizing Receiver To List And Market 410 Eddy Street, San Francisco, California For Sale; And Confirming Sale Procedures
Off Calendar Apr-14-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Robert Kaplan, 415.398.8080 of Jeffer Mangles Butler & Mitchell LLP appearing via Court call for Plaintiff United Business Bank.
Kevin Siner, 415.590.4823, Superior Court Receiver appearing via Zoom.

Hearing held. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF UNITED BUSINESS BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Instructing And Authorizing Receiver To List And Market 410 Eddy Street, San Francisco, California For Sale; And Confirming Sale Procedures is DENIED. Defendants defaulted on December 21, 2022. Plaintiff could have obtained a judgment in this action in department 514 at any time thereafter. There is no indication in the register of actions that Plaintiff attempted to or scheduled a hearing to obtain a judgment even though several Case Management Orders were issued by department 610 for Plaintiff to obtain a default judgment by the time of the CMC. No authority is provided for the proposition that the requested relief is necessary and proper in light of the procedural posture of this case. Plaintiff shall proceed with default judgment forthwith to address the pending CMC order. Prevailing party to prepare order. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

nO OTHER APPEARANCES.

Not ARGUED. Discused. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

UNITED BUSINESS BANKS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE 1. APPOINTMENT OF RECEIVER AND PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IN AID OF RECEIVER; AND 2. TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER is OFF CALENDAR. No valid proof of service on file. POS on file purports to have accomplished service on a corporate defendant by serving someone not identified as an agent for process of service or corporate officer. No explanation or authority provided for service on "other: LLC" in paragraph 6 of the form POS, which requires a CCP section to be listed: "under the following Code of Civil Procedure section."

Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: W. Trupek, Not reported (D501)
